user_message: I need you to process this support ticket from Iron Creek Mining. There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 08:31 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining security posture

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with security posture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 16:02 EST
Subject: security posture - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 14:19 EST
Subject: security posture - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-D trap (Skips current-coverage check (proposes duplicate opportunity)); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-D failure mode (Skips current-coverage check (proposes duplicate opportunity))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-D -- Skips current-coverage check (proposes duplicate opportunity)
